name: Fold

unions.name: Fold
unions.comment: Uses an input `a` to accumulate over `b`.

values.name: arrayLeft
values.comment: Starting from the left, fold each element of the array onto 'b'.
values.type: Fold.Fold a b -> Fold.Fold (Array.Array a) b

values.name: arrayRight
values.comment: Starting from the right, fold each element of the array onto `b`.
values.type: Fold.Fold a b -> Fold.Fold (Array.Array a) b

values.name: custom
values.comment: Construct a `Fold` that uses `a` to accumulate over `b`.

values.name: failed
values.comment: Apply a fold only if the filter fails.
values.type: Filter.Filter a -> Fold.Fold a b -> Fold.Fold a b

values.name: list
values.comment: Cons an element onto the list.

values.name: listLeft
values.comment: Starting from the left, fold each element of the list onto `b`.
values.type: Fold.Fold a b -> Fold.Fold (List.List a) b

values.name: listRight
values.comment: Starting from the right, fold each element of the list onto 'b'.
values.type: Fold.Fold a b -> Fold.Fold (List.List a) b

values.name: maybe
values.comment:
If the input is `Just`, fold over it.

merge (maybe list) [ 2, 3 ] (Just 1)
-- [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ]

merge (maybe string) "hello" Nothing
-- "hello"
values.type: Fold.Fold a b -> Fold.Fold (Maybe.Maybe a) b

values.name: merge
values.comment: Evaluate your fold for a given accumulator and input.
values.type: Fold.Fold a b -> b -> a -> b

values.name: passed
values.comment: Apply a fold only if the filter passes.
values.type: Filter.Filter a -> Fold.Fold a b -> Fold.Fold a b

values.name: result
values.comment: Fold `a` onto `b` if `Result e a` is `Ok`.
values.type: Fold.Fold a b -> Fold.Fold (Result.Result e a) b

values.name: resultError
values.comment: Fold 'e' onto 'b' if `Result e a` is `Err`.
values.type: Fold.Fold e b -> Fold.Fold (Result.Result e b) b

values.name: string
values.comment: Cons a character onto the string.

values.name: stringLeft
values.comment: Starting from the left, fold each character in the string onto `b`.
values.type: Fold.Fold Char.Char b -> Fold.Fold String.String b

values.name: stringRight
values.comment: Starting from the right, fold each character in the string onto `b`.
values.type: Fold.Fold Char.Char b -> Fold.Fold String.String b

values.name: wrap
values.comment: Apply a transformation to input of a `Fold`.
values.type: (c -> a) -> Fold.Fold a b -> Fold.Fold c b

name: Reduce

aliases.name: Reduce
aliases.comment: Combines two of the same thing. Equivalent to a `Fold` where the accumulator and input share the same type.

values.name: and
values.comment: Pass the filter if both filters pass.

values.name: array
values.comment: Append the right array to the left array.

values.name: arrayLeft
values.comment: Reduce an array from the left.
values.type: Reduce.Reduce a -> Array.Array a -> Maybe.Maybe a

values.name: arrayRight
values.comment: Reduce an array from the right.
values.type: Reduce.Reduce a -> Array.Array a -> Maybe.Maybe a

values.name: collect
values.comment:
Reduce over a collection. The initial state is split off of the collection.
The accumulator is the reducer's left input.
values.type: Split.Splitter b a -> (Reduce.Reduce b -> Fold.Fold a b) -> Reduce.Reduce b -> a -> Maybe.Maybe b

values.name: combine
values.comment: Combine two values with a reducer.

values.name: custom
values.comment: Construct a reducer from the given function, where the first and second arguments are the left and right inputs respectively.

values.name: fAdd
values.comment: Add the right float to the left float.

values.name: fDiv
values.comment: Divide the left float by the right float.

values.name: fMul
values.comment: Multiply the right float by the left float.

values.name: fPow
values.comment: Put the left float to the power of the right float.

values.name: fSub
values.comment: Subtract the right float from the left float.

values.name: flip
values.comment: Flip the left and right inputs to a reducer.

values.name: iAdd
values.comment: Add the right int to the left int.

values.name: iDiv
values.comment: Divide the left integer by the right integer.

values.name: iMul
values.comment: Multiply the right integer by the left integer.

values.name: iPow
values.comment: Put the left integer to the power of the right integer.

values.name: iSub
values.comment: Subtract the right int from the left int.

values.name: list
values.comment: Append the right list to the left list.

values.name: listLeft
values.comment: Reduce a list from the left.
values.type: Reduce.Reduce a -> List.List a -> Maybe.Maybe a

values.name: max
values.comment: Keep the larger input, according to the given sorter.

values.name: maybe
values.comment: Keep all `Just` values, reducing if both are present.

values.name: min
values.comment: Keep the smaller input, according to the given sorter.

values.name: or
values.comment: Pass the filter if one filter passes.

values.name: result
values.comment: Keep all `Ok` values, reducing if both are present.
values.type: Reduce.Reduce e -> Reduce.Reduce a -> Reduce.Reduce (Result.Result e a)

values.name: resultError
values.comment: Keep all `Err` values, reducing if both are present.
values.type: Reduce.Reduce e -> Reduce.Reduce a -> Reduce.Reduce (Result.Result e a)

values.name: sorter
values.comment: Sort by both the left and right filter, deferring to the left.

values.name: string
values.comment: Append the right string to the left string.

values.name: stringLeft
values.comment: Reduce a string from the left.

values.name: wrap
values.comment: Apply an operation to the right input of a reducer.

name: Split

unions.name: Splitter
unions.comment: Tries to split an element `a` off of `b`.

values.name: arrayEnd
values.comment: Split off the end of an array.

values.name: arrayStart
values.comment: Split off the start of an array.

values.name: custom
values.comment: Create a splitter that tries to take an element `a` off of `b`.
values.type: (b -> Maybe.Maybe ( a, b )) -> Split.Splitter a b

values.name: listHead
values.comment: Split off the head of a list.

values.name: stringHead
values.comment: Split off the head of a string.

values.name: take
values.comment: Try to split an item off a collection, applying the given function on success.
values.type: (a -> b -> c) -> Split.Splitter a b -> b -> Maybe.Maybe c
